name: high-risk-change
description: Billing payment-method update — high sensitivity, non-trivial scope and complexity. riskContribution 72 => band 'high'.

pr.title: Migrate payment-method storage to Stripe SetupIntent flow
pr.author: morgan
pr.description: Replaces the legacy card-token save flow with Stripe's SetupIntent API so we can store payment methods for future off-session charges. Updates the billing service, the webhook handler that confirms the SetupIntent, and the customer settings page.

agentResponse.summary: This change touches the billing payment-method flow directly, including Stripe SetupIntent creation, webhook signature verification, and database upsert of the default payment method. Sensitivity is very high (payment data, off-session charges). Scope spans multiple packages and complexity includes async error paths and Stripe API state transitions. riskContribution is 72.

findings.title: Payment-method upsert runs inside webhook handler without idempotency guard
findings.detail: confirmSetupIntent() calls db.paymentMethods.upsert() on every webhook delivery. Stripe may deliver the same event more than once; without an idempotency key or deduplication check this can overwrite a newer default payment method.

findings.title: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY read directly from process.env without validation
findings.detail: Both payment-methods.ts and webhooks.ts construct a Stripe client with a non-null assertion on process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY. A missing env var will throw a runtime error rather than a clear startup failure.
